qa_pairs.question: A US TV network for young children had a daily feature to wish viewers happy birthday. Their names and ages would appear on a crawler text banner at the bottom of the screen. One day, every age happened to be the same. How is that possible?
qa_pairs.answer: It was February 29th on a leap year, so all the birthday children shown were the same age.

qa_pairs.question: In 1946, the team running John F. Kennedy's local election campaign persuaded a janitor called Joe Russo to run against him, even though he had no political experience. Why?
qa_pairs.answer: To confuse voters by putting a second candidate with the same name as JFK's opponent, Joe Russo, on the ballot and split his votes.

qa_pairs.question: There is a statue of Winston Churchill outside the British Embassy in Washington, DC. The positioning of the statue is very precise, due to something about Churchill's background. What is it?
qa_pairs.answer: Because Churchill had an American mother and a British father, the statue is placed with one foot on American soil and one on British embassy soil.

qa_pairs.question: In 1963, Heinz Meixner wanted to drive his girlfriend, Margarete Thurau, back home. In order to do so, he had to take the windshield off his rented sports car. Why?
qa_pairs.answer: He removed the windshield so the low sports car could carry his girlfriend under the barrier at Checkpoint Charlie and smuggle her from East Berlin to West Berlin.

qa_pairs.question: In some parking locations, a driver may be required to use a cardboard disc to indicate their time of arrival. How did ingenious Italians find a way of greatly extending their allowed time without anyone helping them?
qa_pairs.answer: They attached an analogue clock mechanism to the back of the parking disc so it would automatically keep advancing the displayed arrival time.

qa_pairs.question: On the tiny volcanic island of Manam Motu, off the coast of Papua New Guinea, nobody speaks of north, south, east, or west. Why?
qa_pairs.answer: Because they use the volcano at the island's center as their reference point, with directions meaning inland, toward the sea, clockwise, and counterclockwise instead of compass points.

qa_pairs.question: In printing terminology, what are also known as shrieks, bangs, and screamers?
